author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm hath an inscription, that is, a title, “There have failed the hymns of David, the son of Jesse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Psalm of Asaph himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So many Psalms we have on the titles whereof is written the name David, nowhere there is added, “son of Jesse,” except in this alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which we must believe hath not been done to no purpose, nor capriciously.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For everywhere God doth make intimations to us, and to the understanding thereof doth invite the godly study of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “there have failed the hymns of David, the son of Jesse”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hymns are praises of God accompanied with singing: hymns are songs containing the praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there be praise, and it be not of God, it is no hymn: if there be praise, and God’s praise, and it be not sung, it is no hymn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It must needs then, if it be a hymn, have these three things, both praise, and that of God, and singing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “there have failed the hymns”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There have failed the praises which are sung unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seemeth to tell of a thing painful, and so to speak deplorable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that singeth praise, not only praiseth, but only praiseth with gladness: he that singeth praise, not only singeth, but also loveth him of whom he singeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In praise, there is the speaking forth of one confessing; in singing, the affection of one loving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There have failed” then “the hymns of David,” he saith: and he hath added, “the son of Jesse.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For David was king of Israel, son of Jesse, at a certain time of the Old Testament, at which time the New Testament was therein hidden, like fruit in a root.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou seek fruit in a root, thou wilt not find, and yet dost thou not find any fruit in the branches, except that which hath gone forth from the root.…And in like manner as Christ Himself to be born after the flesh was hidden in the root, that is in the seed of the Patriarchs, and at a certain time must be revealed, as at the fruit appearing, according as it is written, “there hath flourished a shoot from the root of Jesse:” so also the New Testament itself which is in Christ, in those former times was hidden, being known to the Prophets alone, and to the very few godly men, not by the manifestation of things present, but by the revelation of things future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what meaneth it, brethren (to mention but one thing), that Abraham sending his faithful servant to espouse a wife for his only son, maketh him swear to him, and in the oath saith to him, “Put thy hand under my thigh, and swear”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was there in the thigh of Abraham, where he put his hand in swearing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was there there, except that which even then was promised to him, “In thy seed shall be blessed all nations”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Under the name of thigh, flesh is signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the flesh of Abraham, through Isaac and Jacob, and not to mention many names, through Mary was our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that the root was in the Patriarchs, how shall we show?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us question Paul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gentiles now believing in Christ, and desiring as it were to boast over the Jews who crucified Christ; although also from that same people there came another wall, meeting in the corner, that is, in Christ Himself, the wall of uncircumcision, that is, of the Gentiles, coming from a different quarter: when, I say, the nations were lifting up themselves, he doth thus depress them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if thou,” he saith, “being cut out of the natural wild olive, hast been graffed in among them, do not boast against the branches: for if thou boastest, thou dost not bear the root, but the root thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he speaketh of certain branches broken off from the root of the Patriarchs because of unbelief, and the wild olive therein graffed in, that it might be partaker of the fatness of the olive, that is, the Church coming out of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who doth graff the wild olive on the olive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The olive is wont to be graffed on the wild olive; the wild olive on the olive we never saw.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whosoever may have done so will find no berries but those of the wild olive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which is graffed in, the same groweth, and of that kind the fruit is found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is not found the fruit of the root but of the graft.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle showing that God did this thing by His Omnipotence, namely, that the wild olive should be graffed into the root of the olive, and should not bear wild berries, but olive—ascribing it to the Omnipotence of God, the Apostle saith this, “If thou hast been cut out of the natural wild olive and against nature hast been graffed into a good olive, do not boast,” he saith, “against the branches.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the time then of the Old Testament, brethren, the promises from our God to that carnal people were earthly and temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was promised an earthly kingdom, there was promised that land into which they were also led, after being delivered from Egypt: by Jesus son of Nave they were led into the land of promise, where also earthly Jerusalem was builded, where David reigned: they received the land, after being delivered from Egypt, by passing through the Red Sea.…Such were also those promises, which were not to endure, through which however were figured future promises which were to endure, so that all that course of temporal promises was a figure and a sort of prophecy of things future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly when that kingdom was failing, where reigned David, the son of Jesse, that is, one that was a man, though a Prophet, though holy, because he saw and foresaw Christ to come, of whose seed also after the flesh He was to be born: nevertheless a man, nevertheless not yet Christ, nevertheless not yet our King Son of God, but king David son of Jesse: because then that kingdom was to fail, through the receiving of which kingdom at that time God was praised by carnal men; for this thing alone they esteemed a great matter, namely, that they were delivered temporally from those by whom they were being oppressed, and that they had escaped from persecuting enemies through the Red Sea, and had been led through the desert, and had found country and kingdom: for this alone they praised God, not yet perceiving the thing which God was designing beforehand and promising in these figures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the failing therefore of those things for which the carnal people, over whom reigned that David, was praising God, “there failed the hymns of David,” not the Son of God, but the “son of Jesse.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whose voice is the Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is Asaph?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As we find in interpretations from the Hebrew language into the Greek, and those again translated to us from the Greek into the Latin, Asaph is interpreted Synagogue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the voice therefore of the Synagogue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when thou hast heard Synagogue, do not forthwith abhor it, as if it were the murderer of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Synagogue was indeed the murderer of the Lord, no man doubteth it: but remember, that from the Synagogue were the rams whereof we are the sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence it is said in a Psalm, “Bring ye to the Lord the sons of rams.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What rams are thence?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter, John, James, Andrew, Bartholomew, and the rest of the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence also he too at first Saul, afterwards Paul: that is, at first proud, afterwards humble.…Therefore even Paul came to us from the Synagogue, and Peter and the other Apostles from the Synagogue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when thou hast heard the voice of the Synagogue, do not look to the deserving thereof, but observe the offspring.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is speaking therefore in this Psalm, the Synagogue, after the failing of the hymns of David, the son of Jesse that is, after the failing of things temporal, through which God was wont to be praised by the carnal people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why did these fail, except in order that others might be sought for?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That there might be sought for what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it things which were not there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but things which were there being hidden in figures: not which were not yet there, but which there as it were in a sort were concealed in certain secret things of mysteries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These,” saith the Apostle himself, “were our figures.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was the Synagogue therefore, that is, they that there worshipped God after a godly sort, but yet for the sake of earthly things, for the sake of these present things (for there are ungodly men who seek the blessings of present things from demons: but this people was on this account better than the Gentiles, because although it were blessings present and temporal, yet they sought them from the One God, who is the Creator of all things both spiritual and corporal).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore those godly men after the flesh were observing—that is that Synagogue which was made up of good men, men for the time good, not spiritual men, such as were the Prophets therein, such as were the few that understood the kingdom heavenly, eternal—that Synagogue, I say, observed what things it received from God, and what things God promised to that people, abundance of things earthly, land, peace, earthly felicity: but in all these things were figures, and they not perceiving what was there concealed in things figured, thought that God gave this for a great matter, and had nothing better to give to men loving Him and serving Him: they remarked and saw certain sinners, ungodly, blasphemers, servants of demons, sons of the Devil, living in great naughtiness and pride, yet abounding in such things earthly, temporal, for which sort of things they were serving God themselves: and there sprang up a most evil thought in the heart, which made the feet to totter, and almost slip out of God’s way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And behold this thought was in the people of the Old Testament: I would it be not in our carnal brethren, when now openly there is being proclaimed the felicity of the New Testament.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How good is the God of Israel!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To men right in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To men perverse what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perverse He seemeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also in another Psalm He saith: “With a holy man holy Thou shalt be, and with the innocent man innocent Thou shalt be, and with the perverse man perverse Thou shalt be.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, perverse Thou shalt be with the perverse man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perverse the perverse man shall think Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that by any means God is made perverse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it: what He is, He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in like manner as the sun appeareth mild to one having clear, sound, healthy, strong eyes, but against weak eyes doth dart hard spears, so to say; the former looking at it it doth invigorate, the latter it doth torture, though not being itself changed, but the man being changed: so when thou shalt have begun to be perverse, and to thee God shall seem to be perverse, thou art changed, not He.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That therefore to thee will be punishment which to good men is joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calling to mind this thing, saith, “How good is the God of Israel to men right in heart!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what to thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But my feet were almost moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When were the feet moved, except when the heart was not right?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence was the heart not right?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear: “My steps were well nigh overthrown.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he hath meant by “almost,” the same he hath meant by “well nigh:” and what he hath meant by “my feet were almost moved,” the same he hath meant by “my steps were overthrown.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Almost my feet were moved, almost my steps were overthrown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moved were the feet: but whence were the feet moved and the steps overthrown?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moved were the feet to going astray, overthrown were the steps to falling: not entirely, but “almost.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already I was going to stray, I had not gone: already I was falling, I had not fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why even this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I was jealous,” he saith, “in the case of sinners, looking on the peace of sinners” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I observed sinners, I saw them to have peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Temporal, transient, falling, and earthly: but yet such as I also was desiring of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I saw them that served not God to have that which I desired in order that I might serve God: and my feet were moved and my steps were almost overthrown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why sinners have this, he saith briefly: “Because there is no avoidance of their death, and there is a firmament in their scourge” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now I have perceived, he saith, why they have peace, and flourish on the earth; because of their death there is no avoidance, because death sure and eternal doth await them, which neither doth avoid them, nor can they avoid it, “because there is no avoidance of their death, and there is a firmament in their scourge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And there is a firmament in their scourge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For their scourge is not temporal, but firm for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because of these evil things then which are to be to them eternal, now what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the labours of men they are not, and with men they shall not be scourged” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth not even the devil himself escape scourging with men, for whom nevertheless an eternal punishment is being prepared?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore on this account what do these men, while they are not scourged, while they labour not with men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore,” he saith “there hath holden them pride” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe these men, proud, undisciplined; observe the bull, devoted for a victim, suffered to stray at liberty; and to damage whatever he may, even up to the day of his slaughter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now it is a good thing, brethren, that we should hear in the very words of a prophet of this bull as it were, whereof I have spoken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus of him the Scripture doth make mention in another place: he saith that they are, as it were, made ready as for a victim, and that they are spared for an evil liberty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore,” he saith, “there hath holden them pride.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “there hath holden them pride”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have been clothed about with their iniquity and ungodliness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not said, covered; but, “clothed about,” on all sides covered up with their ungodliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly miserable, they neither see nor are seen, because they are clothed about; and the inward parts of them are not seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whosoever could behold the inward parts of evil men, that are as it were happy for a time, whosoever could see their torturing consciences, whosoever could examine their souls racked with such mighty perturbations of desires and fears, would see them to be miserable even when they are called happy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because “they are clothed about with their iniquity and ungodliness,” they see not; but neither are they seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Spirit knew them, that saith these words concerning them: and we ought to examine such men with the same eye as that wherewith we know that we see, if there is taken from our eyes the covering of ungodliness.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first these men are being described.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall go forth as if out of fat their iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7).…A poor beggar committeth a theft; out of leanness hath gone forth the iniquity: but when a rich man aboundeth in so many things, why doth he plunder the things of others?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the former the iniquity out of leanness, of the other out of fatness, hath gone forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore to the lean man when thou sayest, Why hast thou done this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humbly afflicted and abject he replieth, Need hath compelled me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hast thou not feared God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Want was urgent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say to a rich man, Why doest thou these things, and fearest not God?—supposing thee to be great enough to be able to say it—see if he even deigneth to hear; see if even against thyself there will not go forth iniquity out of his fatness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now they declare war with their teachers and reprovers, and become enemies of them that speak the truth, having been long accustomed to be coaxed with the words of flatterers, being of tender ear, of unsound heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who would say to a rich man, Thou hast ill done in robbing other men’s goods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or perchance if any man shall have dared to speak, and he is such a man as he could not withstand, what doth he reply?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that he saith is in contempt of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he is proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he is fat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he is devoted for a victim.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have passed over unto purpose of heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here within they have passed over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “they have passed over”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have crossed over the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “they have passed over”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have exceeded the bounds of mankind, men like the rest they think not themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have passed over, I say, the bounds of mankind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou sayest to such a man, Thy brother this beggar is; when thou sayest to such a man, Thy brother this poor man is; the same parents ye have had, Adam and Eve: do not heed thy haughtiness, do not heed the vapour unto which thou hast been elevated; although an establishment waiteth about thee, although countless gold and silver, although a marbled house doth contain thee, although fretted ceilings cover thee, thou and the poor man together have for covering that roof of the universe, the sky; but thou art different from the poor man in things not thine own, added to thee from without: thyself see in them, not them in thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe thyself, how thou art in relation to the poor man; thyself, not that which thou hast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For why dost thou despise thy brother?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the bowels of your mothers ye were both naked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Forsooth, even when ye shall have departed this life, and these bodies shall have rotted, when the soul hath been breathed forth, let the bones of the rich and poor man be distinguished!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am speaking of the equality of condition, of that very lot of mankind, wherein all men are born: for both here doth a man become rich, and a poor man will not alway be here: and as a rich man doth not come rich, so neither doth he depart rich; the very same is the entrance of both, and like is the departure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I add, that perchance ye will change conditions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now everywhere the Gospel is being preached: observe a certain poor man full of sores, who was lying before the gate of a rich man, and was desiring to be filled with crumbs, which used to fall from the table of the rich man; observe also that likeness of thine who was clothed with purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It chanced, I say, for that poor man to die, and to be borne by the Angels into the bosom of Abraham: but the other died and was buried; for the other’s burial perchance no one cared.…Brethren, how great was the toil of the poor man!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of how long duration were the luxuries of the rich man!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the condition which they have received in exchange is everlasting.…Deservedly too late he will say, “Send Lazarus,” “let him tell even my brethren;” since to himself there is not granted the fruit of repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not that repentance is not given, but everlasting will be the repentance, and no salvation after repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore these men “have passed over unto purpose of heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have thought and have spoken spitefulness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But men do speak spitefulness even with fear: but these men how?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Iniquity on high they have spoken.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only they have spoken iniquity; but even openly, in the hearing of all, proudly; “I will do it;” “I will show you;” “thou shalt know with whom thou hast to do;” “I will not let thee live.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou mightest have but thought such things, not have given utterance to them!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Within the chambers of thought at least the evil desire might have been confined, he might have at least restrained it within his thought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is he perchance lean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall go forth as if out of fatness the iniquity of them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Iniquity on high they have spoken.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have set against Heaven their mouth, and their tongue hath passed over above the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this, “hath passed over above the earth” is, they pass over all earthly things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it to pass over all earthly things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not think of himself as a man that can die suddenly, when he is speaking; he doth menace as if he were alway to live: his thought doth transcend earthly frailty, he knoweth not with what sort of vessel he is enwrapped; he knoweth not what hath been written in another place concerning such men: “His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return unto his earth, in that day shall perish all his thoughts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these men not thinking of their last day, speak pride, and unto Heaven they set their mouth, they transcend the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a robber were not to think of his last day, that is, the last day of his trial, when sent to prison, nothing would be more monstrous than he: and yet he might escape.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither dost thou flee to escape death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certain will that day be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the long time which thou hast to live?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much is the long time which hath an end, even if it were a long time?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this there is added that it is nought: and the very thing which is called long time is not a long time, and is uncertain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth he not think of this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he hath set against Heaven his mouth, and his tongue hath passed over above the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And full days shall be found in them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore there shall return hither My people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now Asaph himself is returning hither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saw these things abound to unrighteous men, he saw them abound to proud men: he is returning to God, and is beginning to inquire and discuss.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When full days shall be found in them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “full days”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But when there came the fulness of time, God sent His Son.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the very fulness of time, when He came to teach men that things temporal should be despised, that they should not esteem as a great matter whatever object evil men covet, that they should suffer whatever evil men fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He became the way, He recalled us to inward thought, admonished us of what should be sought of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And see from what thought reacting upon itself, and in a manner recalling the waves of its impulse, he doth pass over unto choosing true things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they said, How hath God known, and is there knowledge in the Most High?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See through what thought they pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold unjust men are happy, God doth not care for things human.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth He indeed know what we do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what things are being said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are inquiring, brethren, “How hath God known,” etc. (no longer let Christians say it).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how doth it appear to thee that God knoweth not, and that there is no knowledge in the Most High?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He replieth, “Lo! themselves they are sinners, and in the world they have gotten abundant riches” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both sinners they are, and in the world they have gotten abundant riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He confessed that he willed not to be a sinner in order that he might have riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A carnal soul for things visible and earthly would have sold its justice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of justice is that which is retained for the sake of gold, as if gold were a more precious thing than justice herself, or as if when a man denieth the deposit of another man’s goods, he to whom he denied them should suffer a greater loss, than he that denieth them to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former doth lose a garment, the latter fidelity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lo! they are themselves sinners, and in the world they have gotten abundant riches.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On this account therefore God knoweth not, and on this account there is no knowledge in the Most High.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I said, therefore without cause I have justified my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that I serve God, and have not these things; they serve him not, and they abound in these things: “therefore without cause I have justified my heart, and have washed among the innocent my hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This without cause I have done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the reward of my good life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the wage of my service?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I live well and am in need; and the unjust man doth abound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have washed among the innocent my hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And I have been scourged all the day long” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From me the scourges of God do not impart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I serve well, and I am scourged; he serveth not, and is honoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath proposed to himself a great question.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The soul is disturbed, the soul doth pass over things which are to pass away unto despising things earthly and to desiring things eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a passage of the soul herself in this thought; where she doth toss in a sort of tempest she will reach the harbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is with her as it is with sick persons, who are less violently sick, when recovery is far off: when recovery is at hand they are in higher fever; physicians call it the “critical accession” through which they pass to health: greater fever is there, but leading to health: greater heat, but recovery is at hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also is this man enfevered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these are dangerous words, brethren, offensive, and almost blasphemous, “How hath God known?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is why I say, “and almost;” He hath not said, God hath not known: he hath not said, there is no knowledge in the Most High: but as if inquiring, hesitating, doubting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the same as he said a little before, “My steps were almost overthrown.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not affirm it, but the very doubt is dangerous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through danger he is passing to health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear now the health: “Therefore in vain I have justified my heart, and have washed among the innocent my hands: and I have been scourged all the day long, and my chastening was in the morning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Chastening is correction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that is being chastened is being corrected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in the morning”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not deferred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That of the ungodly is being deferred, mine is not deferred: the former is too late or is not at all; mine is in the morning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I said, I shall declare thus; behold, the generation of Thy sons I have reprobated” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 15): that is, I will teach thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How wilt thou teach? that there is no knowledge in the Most High, that God doth not know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou propound this opinion, that without cause men live justly who do live justly; that a just man hath lost his service, because God doth more show favour to evil men, or else He doth care for no one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou tell this, declare this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth restrain himself by an authority repressing him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What authority?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man wisheth some time to break out in this sentiment: but he is recalled by the Scriptures directing us alway to live well, saying, that God doth care for things human, that He maketh a distinction between a godly man and an ungodly man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore this man also wishing to put forth this sentiment, doth recollect himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have reprobated the generation of Thy sons.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If I shall declare thus, the generation of just men I shall reprobate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As also some copies have it, “Behold, the generation of thy sons with which I have been in concert:” that is, with which consisting of Thy sons I have been in concert; that is, with which I have agreed, to which I have been conformed: I have been out of time with all, if so I teach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he doth sing in concert who giveth the tune together; but he that giveth not the tune together doth not sing in concert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Am I to say something different from that which Abraham said, from that which Isaac said, from that which Jacob said, from that which the Prophets said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all they said that God doth care for things human, am I to say that He careth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there greater wisdom in me than in them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Greater understanding in me than in them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A most wholesome authority hath called back his thought from ungodliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That he might not reprobate, he did what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I undertook to know” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May God be with him in order that he may know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Meanwhile, brethren, from a great fall he is being withheld, when he doth not presume that he already knoweth, but hath undertaken to know that which he knew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For but now he was willing to appear as if knowing, and to declare that God hath no care of things human.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this hath come to be a most naughty and ungodly doctrine of unrighteous men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Know, brethren, that many men dispute and say that God careth not for things human, that by chances all things are ruled, or that our wills have been made subject to the stars, that each one is not dealt with according to his deserts, but by the necessity of his stars,—an evil doctrine, an impious doctrine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto these thoughts was going that man whose feet were almost moved, and whose steps were all but overthrown, into this error he was going; but because he was not in tune with the generation of the sons of God, he undertook to know, and condemned the knowledge wherein with God’s just men he agreed not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what he saith let us hear; how that he undertook to know, and was helped, and learned something, and declared it to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I undertook,” he saith, “to know.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In this labour is before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly a great labour; to know in what manner both God doth care for things human, and it is well with evil men, and good men labour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is the importance of the question; therefore, “and this labour is before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it were there is standing in my face a sort of wall, but thou hast the voice of a Psalm, “In my God I shall pass over the wall.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17. …And he hath done this; for he saith how long labour is before him; “until I enter into the sanctuary of God, and understand upon the last things” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great thing it is, brethren: now for a long time I labour, he saith, and before my face I see a sort of insuperable labour, to know in what manner both God is just, and doth care for things human, and is not unjust because men sinning and doing wicked actions have happiness on this earth; but the godly and men serving God are wasted ofttimes in trials and in labours; a great difficulty it is to know this, but only “until I enter into the Sanctuary of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the Sanctuary what is presented to thee, in order that thou mayest solve this question?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I understand,” he saith, “upon the last things:” not present things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, he saith, from the Sanctuary of God stretch out mine eye unto the end, I pass over present things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that which is called the human race, all that mass of mortality is to come to the balance, is to come to the scale, thereon will be weighed the works of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things now a cloud doth enfold: but to God are known the merits of each severally.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I understand,” he saith, “upon the last things:” but not of myself; for before me there is labour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence “may I understand upon the last things”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me enter into the Sanctuary of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that place then he understood also the reason why these men now are happy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To wit, “because of deceitfulness Thou hast set upon them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because deceitful they are, that is fraudulent; because deceitful they are, they suffer deceits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, because fraudulent they are they suffer a fraud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They desire to play a fraud upon mankind in all their naughtinesses, they themselves also suffer a fraud, in choosing earthly good things, and in forsaking the eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, in their very playing off a fraud they suffer a fraud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that which but now I said, brethren, “What manner of wit hath he who to gain a garment doth lose his fidelity?” hath he whose garment he hath taken suffered a fraud, or he that is smitten with so great a loss?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a garment is more precious than fidelity, the former doth suffer the greater loss: but if incomparably good faith doth surpass the whole world, the latter shall seem to have sustained the loss of a garment; but to the former is said, “What doth it profit a man if he gain the whole world, but suffer the loss of his own soul?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore what hath befallen them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of deceitfulness Thou hast set for them: Thou didst throw them down while they were being exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not said, Thou didst throw them down because they were lifted up: not as it were after that they were lifted up Thou didst throw them down; but in their very lifting up they were thrown down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus to be lifted up is already to fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How have they become a desolation suddenly?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is wondering at them, understanding unto the last things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have vanished.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly like smoke, which while it mounteth upward, doth vanish, so they have vanished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth he say, “They have vanished”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the manner of one who understandeth the last things: “they have perished because of their iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like as the dream of one rising up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How have they vanished?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As vanisheth the dream of one rising up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fancy a man in sleep to have seen himself find treasures; he is a rich man, but only until he awaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like as the dream of one rising up:” so they have vanished, like the dream of one awaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is sought then and it is not: there is nothing in the hands, nothing in the bed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A poor man he went to sleep, a rich man in sleep he became: had he not awoke, he were a rich man: he woke up, he found the care which he had lost while sleeping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these men shall find the misery which they had prepared for themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they shall have awoke from this life, that thing doth pass away which was grasped as if in sleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like as the dream of one rising up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that there might not be said, “What then? a small thing doth their glory seem to thee, a small thing doth their state seem to thee, small things seem to thee inscriptions, images, statues, distinctions, troops of clients?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord,” he saith, “in Thy city their image Thou shalt bring to nothing.”…He hath taken away the pride of rich men, he giveth counsel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if they were saying, We are rich men, thou dost forbid us to be proud, dost prohibit us from boasting of the parade of our riches: what then are we to do with these riches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it come to this, that there is nothing which they may do therewith?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be they rich,” he saith, “in good works; let them readily distribute, communicate.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what doth this profit?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them treasure unto themselves a good foundation for the future, that they may lay hold of true life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where ought they to lay up treasure for themselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that place whereunto he set his eye, when entering into the Sanctuary of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let there shudder all our rich brethren, abounding in money, gold, silver, household, honours, let them shudder at that which but now hath been said, “Thou shalt bring to nothing their image.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are they not worthy to suffer these things, to wit that God bring to nothing their image in His city, because also they have themselves brought to nothing the image of God in their earthly city?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because my heart was delighted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is saying with what things he is tempted: “because my heart was delighted,” he saith, “my reins also were changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When those temporal things delighted me, my reins were changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It may also be understood thus: “because my heart was delighted” in God, “my reins also were changed, that is, my lusts were changed, and I became wholly chaste.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My reins were changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hear how.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I was brought unto nothing, and I knew not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, the very man, who now say these things of rich men, once longed for such things: therefore “even I was brought to nothing” when my steps were almost overthrown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I was brought unto nothing, and I knew not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must not therefore despair even of them, against whom I was saying such things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “I knew not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As it were a beast I became to Thee, and I am alway with Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a great difference between this man and others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He became as it were a beast in longing for earthly things, when being brought to nothing he knew not things eternal: but he departed not from his God, because he did not desire these things of demons, of the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this I have already brought to your notice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The voice is from the Synagogue, that is, from that people which served not idols.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A beast indeed I became, when desiring from my God things earthly: but I never departed from That my God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then, though having become a beast, I departed not from my God, there followeth, “Thou hast held the hand of my right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not said my right hand, but “the hand of my right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the hand of the right hand it is, a hand hath a hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The hand Thou hast held of my right hand,” in order that Thou mightest conduct me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hath he put hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we say that a man hath that in his hand which he hath in his power: just as the devil said to God concerning Job, “Lay to Thine hand, and take away the things which he hath.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, lay to Thine hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Put forth power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hand of God he hath called the power of God: as hath been written in another place, “death and life are in the hands of the tongue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath the tongue hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is, in the hands of the tongue?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the power of the tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, in the power of the tongue?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of thy mouth thou shalt be justified, and out of thy mouth thou shalt be condemned.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast held,” therefore, “the hand of my right hand,” the power of my right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was my right hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I was alway with Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto the left I was holding, because I became a beast, that is, because there was an earthly concupiscence in me: but the right was mine, because I was alway with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this my right hand Thou hast held the hand, that is, hast directed the power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He gave them power to become sons of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is beginning now to be among the sons of God, belonging to the New Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See in what manner the hand of his right hand was held.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy will Thou hast conducted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in thy will”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in my merits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in Thy will”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the apostle, who was at first a beast longing for things earthly, and living after the Old Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I that at first was a blasphemer, and persecutor, and injurious: but mercy I obtained.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in Thy will”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By the grace of God I am what I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in glory Thou hast taken me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now to what glory he was taken up, and in what glory, who can explain, who can say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us await it, because in the Resurrection it will be, in the last things it will be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he is beginning to think of that same Heavenly felicity, and to reprove himself, because he hath been a beast, and hath longed for things earthly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For what have I in Heaven, and from Thee what have I willed upon earth?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By your voice I see that ye have understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He compared with his earthly will the heavenly reward which he is to receive; he saw what was there being reserved for him; and while thinking and burning at the thought of some ineffable thing, which neither eye hath seen, nor ear heard, nor into the heart of man hath ascended, he hath not said, this or that I have in Heaven, but, “what have I in Heaven?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that thing which I have in Heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what sort is it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And,” since that which I have in heaven doth not pass away, “from Thee what have I willed upon earth?”…Thou reservest, he saith, for me in Heaven riches immortal, even Thyself, and I have willed from Thee on earth that which even ungodly men have, which even evil men have, which even abandoned men have, money, gold, silver, jewels, households, which even many wicked men have: which even many profligate women have, many profligate men: these things as a great matter I have desired of my God upon earth: though my God reserveth Himself for me in Heaven!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart and my flesh hath failed, O God of my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then for me in Heaven hath been reserved, “God of my heart, and my portion is my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us find out our riches, let mankind choose their parts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see men torn with diversity of desires: let some choose war-service, some advocacy, some divers and sundry offices of teaching, some merchandise, some farming, let them take their portions in human affairs: let the people of God cry, “my portion is my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not for a time “my portion;” but “my portion is my God for everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even if I alway have gold, what have I?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even if I did not alway have God, how great a good should I have?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this is added, that He promiseth Himself to me, and He promiseth that I shall have this for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So great a thing I have, and never have it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great felicity: “my portion is God!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For behold and see after what sort He hath loved him; He hath made his heart chaste: “God of my heart, and my portion is God for everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His heart hath become chaste, for nought now God is loved, from Him is not sought any other reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that doth seek any other reward from God, and therefore is willing to serve God, more precious doth make that which he willeth to receive, than Him from whom he willeth to receive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, is there no reward belonging to God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: None except Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The reward belonging to God, is God Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This he loveth, this he esteemeth; if any other thing he shall have loved, the love will not be chaste.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art receding from the Fire immortal, thou wilt grow cold, wilt be corrupted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not recede.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Recede not, it will be thy corruption, it will be thy fornication.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now he is returning, now he is repenting, now he is choosing repentance, now he is saying, “my portion is God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And after what sort is he delighted with that Same, whom he hath chosen for his portion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, they that put themselves afar from Thee shall perish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore departed from God, but not far: for “I have become as it were a beast,” he saith, and “I am alway with Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they have departed afar, because not only things earthly they have desired, but have sought them from demons and the Devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They that put themselves afar from Thee shall perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is it, to become afar from God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast destroyed every man that committeth fornication away from Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this fornication is opposed chaste love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is chaste love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the soul doth love her Bridegroom: what doth she require of Him, from Her Bridegroom whom she loveth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perchance in like manner as women choose for themselves men either as sons-in-law or as bridegrooms: she perchance chooseth riches, and loveth his gold, and estates, and silver and cattle and horses, and household, and the like.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth love Him alone, for nought he doth love Him: because in Him he hath all things, for “by Him were made all things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou doest what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But for me to cleave to God is a good thing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is whole good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will ye have more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I grieve at your willing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, what will ye have more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Than to cleave to God nothing is better, when we shall see Him face to face.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For yet as a stranger I am speaking: “to cleave,” he saith, “to God is a good thing:” but now in my sojourning (for not yet hath come the substance), I have “to put in God my hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long therefore as thou hast not yet cloven, therein put thy hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art wavering, cast forward an anchor to the land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet dost thou cleave by presence, cleave fast by hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To put in God my hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And by doing what here wilt thou put in God thy hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What will be thy business, but to praise Him whom thou lovest, and to make others to be fellow-lovers of Him with thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, if thou shouldest love a charioteer, wouldest thou not carry along other men to love him with thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A lover of a charioteer whithersoever he goeth doth speak of him, in order that as well as he others also may love him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For nought are loved abandoned men, and from God is reward required in order that He may be loved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love thou God for nought, grudge God to no one.…For what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In order that I may tell forth all Thy praises in the courts of the daughter of Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the courts:” for the preaching of God beside the Church is vain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A small thing it is to praise God and to tell forth all His praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the courts of the daughter of Sion tell thou forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make for unity, do not divide the people; but draw them unto one, and make them one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have forgotten how long I have been speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the Psalm being ended, even judging by this closeness, I suppose I have held a long discourse: but it doth not suffice for your zeal; ye are too impetuous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O that with this impetuosity ye would seize upon the kingdom of Heaven.
